summary: Moved collision-related shared types out of `src/melee/ft/types.h` into `src/melee/lb/types.h` so `src/melee/it/types.h` no longer needs to include the full fighter type header. The PR replaces the item header dependency on `ft/types.h` with more appropriate `cm/forward.h`, `lb/types.h`, and localized forward includes, while updating forward declarations for `CollData` and map headers that depended on it.
what_changed: Added `#include "cm/forward.h"` and `#include "lb/types.h"` to `src/melee/it/types.h`, replacing the prior use of `lb/forward.h` plus the heavyweight fighter type include.
Moved `ECBFlagStruct`, `SurfaceData`, `ftECB`, and `struct CollData` from `src/melee/ft/types.h` into `src/melee/lb/types.h` without changing their field layout in the shown diff.
Moved the `CollData` forward declaration from `src/melee/ft/forward.h` to `src/melee/lb/forward.h`.
Changed `src/melee/mp/forward.h` to include `lb/forward.h` instead of `ft/forward.h`, aligning map forward dependencies with the new home of `CollData`.
Added `<dolphin/mtx/forward.h>` to `src/melee/mp/mpisland.h`, apparently to cover matrix/vector forward declarations previously provided transitively.

Added `ft/forward.h` to `src/melee/it/items/itseakneedleheld.h` so its `Fighter_GObj*` API still has the needed fighter forward declarations after `it/types.h` stopped pulling fighter types transitively.
smart_moves: Identified that `CollData` and related collision/environment-box types were not purely fighter-owned and were better placed in the library layer used by both item and map code.
Preserved struct layout and offset comments while relocating definitions, reducing risk of ABI/layout regressions during a header cleanup.

Replaced broad transitive includes with narrower local dependencies: item type users now depend on `lb/types.h` for collision data, while an individual item header includes `ft/forward.h` only where it exposes `Fighter_GObj*`.
Updated forward headers together with full type headers, avoiding stale forward declarations in the fighter namespace after moving `CollData` to `lb`.
decomp_lessons: Do not leave shared engine/collision structs in `ft/types.h` solely because fighter code uses them; place them in the lowest reasonable subsystem header to avoid item and map code depending on all fighter types.

When removing a transitive include, audit headers that were relying on it indirectly and add explicit local includes such as `ft/forward.h` or `<dolphin/mtx/forward.h>` where the declarations are actually needed.

Forward declarations should live in the same subsystem as the concrete type definition. After moving `struct CollData` to `lb/types.h`, `typedef struct CollData CollData;` belongs in `lb/forward.h`, not `ft/forward.h`.

Header dependency cleanup can be decompilation-enabling even without changing C logic: smaller include graphs make type ownership clearer and reduce accidental coupling between fighter, item, map, and library code.
When relocating known structs, keep names, field order, anonymous unions, bitfields, and offset comments intact unless there is explicit evidence to rename or restructure them.
naming_conventions: `CollData` is forward-declared as `typedef struct CollData CollData;` and defined as `struct CollData`, matching the existing non-underscored public type name.
`ECBFlagStruct` keeps its typedef form from `struct _ECBFlagStruct`, preserving the existing underscore-tag/internal-tag convention.
`ftECB` keeps its existing fighter-prefixed typedef even after moving to `lb/types.h`; the PR does not rename it despite the new header location.
`SurfaceData` remains a plain descriptive typedef for map/collision surface information.
Unidentified fields retain offset-style names such as `x34_flags`, `xA4_ecbCurrCorrect`, `env_flags`, `prev_env_flags`, `right_wall`, and `left_wall`; no speculative semantic renames were introduced.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or function matching changes are shown; this PR is a header/type ownership cleanup.
The matching-relevant tactic is preserving the exact `CollData` layout, including bitfield container types, unions, arrays, and offset comments, while moving the declaration to a different header.
Removing transitive includes can expose missing forward declarations; the PR fixes those explicitly instead of reintroducing the broad `ft/types.h` include.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR context.
There is no direct reviewer rationale beyond the PR title and diff; conclusions about intent are based on the include and type relocation changes.
follow_up_queries: Find other headers including `ft/types.h` only for `CollData`, `ftECB`, `ECBFlagStruct`, or `SurfaceData`.
Search for remaining transitive dependencies from `it/types.h` into fighter headers.
Audit whether the `ftECB` name should remain fighter-prefixed now that the type lives in `lb/types.h`, but do not rename without matching/project evidence.
Check map and item headers for missing explicit `<dolphin/mtx/forward.h>` or `ft/forward.h` includes after include-graph cleanup.
Search for `typedef struct CollData CollData;` to ensure the forward declaration is consistently sourced from `lb/forward.h`.
